src/main/java/org/flaad/dsm/client/model.general_idea: Remove the @NoArgsConstructor annotation because the class now uses final fields requiring an all-args constructor.
src/main/java/org/flaad/dsm/client/model.why_exists: Compile error: @NoArgsConstructor cannot generate a constructor for a class with final fields that are not initialized in the declaration.

src/main/java/org/flaad/dsm/client/model.general_idea: Change instance fields to final to enforce immutability and require initialization via the constructor.
src/main/java/org/flaad/dsm/client/model.why_exists: Compile error: Final fields must be definitely assigned in the constructor or at declaration; removing @NoArgsConstructor without making fields final or providing a valid constructor would fail if the class relied on Lombok's default behavior for non-final fields.

src/main/java/org/flaad/dsm/client/model.general_idea: Removed the @NoArgsConstructor annotation because the class now defines a private constructor with arguments and final fields, making a no-arg constructor impossible or undesirable.
src/main/java/org/flaad/dsm/client/model.why_exists: Lombok cannot generate a no-args constructor for a class with final fields that are not initialized in the declaration, and the class explicitly defines a private constructor. Without removing this annotation, compilation fails due to conflicting constructor generation or inability to initialize final fields.

src/main/java/org/flaad/dsm/client/model.general_idea: Removed Jackson @JsonCreator and @JsonProperty annotations as they are no longer needed or compatible with the new Lombok-based field structure.
src/main/java/org/flaad/dsm/client/model.why_exists: The constructor annotated with @JsonCreator is removed entirely; keeping the imports or annotations would cause compilation errors due to missing symbols or unused imports if the logic changes, but primarily the removal of the constructor breaks the previous deserialization contract.

src/main/java/org/flaad/dsm/client/model.general_idea: Removed the explicit constructor for Version, likely relying on Lombok or default behavior.
src/main/java/org/flaad/dsm/client/model.why_exists: The explicit constructor is removed. If code relied on this specific constructor signature for instantiation or Jackson deserialization (via @JsonCreator), it will fail at runtime or compile time if the class is no longer serializable/deserializable in the expected way.
